claimSummary: Philadelphia water and sewer customers are responsible for maintaining and repairing sewer lateral lines connecting the house to the sewer main.
lineLocationNotes: Philadelphia materials describe the lateral as piping connecting the house drain to the public sewer.
homeownerCityCaveat: Official Philadelphia materials still require property-specific verification, especially for shared or private systems.

claimSummary: In Portland, owner-versus-city responsibility depends on whether the problem is in the line from the home to the street or in the City sewer line and curb/right-of-way boundary.
lineLocationNotes: Portland BES and BDS describe owner responsibility from the house toward the curb, with city maintenance from the main to the curb line.
homeownerCityCaveat: Permits, right-of-way work, and shared/party sewer conditions can change the practical answer.

claimSummary: NYC DEP states owners are responsible for maintaining water and sanitary sewer service lines from the property to the mains and offers optional service line protection.
lineLocationNotes: NYC frames service lines as private plumbing outside the property that extends to the main in the street.
homeownerCityCaveat: Optional protection exists, but coverage limitations and eligibility still apply.

claimSummary: Homeowner versus city sewer responsibility varies by jurisdiction and line location, so national pages should present patterns and verification steps rather than universal rules.
lineLocationNotes: Use official utility or city documents to verify the line boundary that matters for a given property.
homeownerCityCaveat: Do not state that the city always pays or that the homeowner always pays without local sourcing.
